name: Lampco
description_short: Lampco FCU is a member-owned credit union serving Indiana communities with checking, savings, loans, and financial services through multiple locations.
description_long:
Lampco Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union operating in Indiana with branches in Anderson, Alexandria, and Pendleton. The organization emphasizes community involvement through its active Volunteer Committee and maintains accessibility standards on its digital platforms. As a federally-insured credit union, Lampco operates under NCUA oversight and provides traditional financial services to its member base.

Lampco offers a comprehensive range of financial products including personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash Back Checking), savings products (Kasasa Saver, Christmas Club, Money Market, IRAs), and share certificates of deposit. The credit union provides multiple loan types: auto loans, home mortgages, HELOCs, personal loans, personal lines of credit, and RV loans. Additional services include mobile banking, online bill pay, eStatements, debit and credit cards, telephone banking, surcharge-free ATM access through Co-Op networks (5,000+ locations nationwide), and overdraft courtesy pay options.

Lampco distinguishes itself through the Co-Op Shared Branch network access, allowing members to conduct banking at thousands of credit unions nationwide. The credit union actively promotes community engagement via its volunteer committee and offers specialized tools like an Auto Shopper platform for vehicle research and financing. The institution also provides investment services through partnerships (GreenPath) and ancillary offerings like TruStage AD&D insurance.

Lampco is best suited for Indiana residents seeking community-focused banking with competitive credit union rates and flexible lending terms. The institution's strength lies in its local presence combined with nationwide access through shared branching. Members should note that as a regional credit union, Lampco has limited physical branch locations compared to major banks, and eligibility to join may be restricted to those meeting membership criteria based on occupation, geography, or association.
services: Personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash Back Checking)
Savings accounts (Kasasa Saver, Member Savings, Christmas Club)
Personal loans and personal lines of credit
Mobile banking and online bill pay
pros: Access to 5,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through Co-Op Shared Branch network
Free Kasasa Cash Back Checking with rewards on debit card purchases
Competitive CD specials marketed as high-return deposit products
Multiple loan options including auto, home, HELOC, personal, and RV financing
Mobile banking and online bill pay available for account management
Community-focused with active volunteer committee supporting local initiatives
AudioEye accessibility optimization for members with assistive technology needs
cons: Limited physical branch locations compared to regional or national banks (only 3 Indiana locations mentioned)
Membership eligibility may be restricted based on occupation, geography, or organizational affiliation
Website does not disclose current APR rates or loan terms for comparison
No mention of premium checking accounts with higher interest rates or enhanced benefits
Limited information about minimum deposit requirements or account opening fees
best_for: Indiana residents in Anderson, Alexandria, or Pendleton seeking community credit union banking
Members prioritizing access to nationwide ATM networks without surcharges
Auto buyers interested in competitive rates and flexible auto loan terms
Customers valuing community involvement and volunteer-driven initiatives
diagnosis: Lampco FCU is best for Indiana residents seeking member-owned credit union benefits including competitive lending rates, nationwide ATM access, and community engagement. The primary caveat is geographic and membership eligibility restrictions—membership may require residency or occupational/organizational affiliation in the service area, and physical branch access is limited to three Indiana locations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
